summary: First end-to-end test of CreatorHub v1 (FastAPI + React). Ran 21 backend pytest cases covering auth, dashboard, analytics, tasks↔calendar atomic sync, team CRUD, permissions enforcement (Tom/Priya/Marcus), brand deals, connections, and AI insights — all 21 PASSED after a small backend fix. Ran Playwright UI suite covering owner login, sidebar rendering, forced-password-change for scheduler+analyst, redirect to /no-access for unauthorized routes, tasks/calendar/team/posts/analytics page loads. UI renders cleanly with zero JS console errors.

minor.issue: Returned 500 due to Mongo ObjectId '_id' mutation leaking into response dict (FastAPI encoder fails). FIXED by excluding '_id' when building response (server.py lines 247, 706). Retested and both now return 200.

ui_bugs.issue: Prev/Next month arrow buttons lack data-testid='calendar-prev' / 'calendar-next' (referenced in spec). Functionality may work but automation hooks missing.

issues: Recharts emits many 'width(-1) and height(-1)' warnings — Responsive container likely has 0 width during first paint (flex collapse). Cosmetic (charts render after resize) but noisy in console.

Analytics 'Weekly views' chart shows bars only for W7 and W8 — remaining weeks render blank because seeded analytics_snapshots only cover last ~30 days. Consider backfilling seed data for 8 weeks or relabeling the card.

action_items: (OPTIONAL) Add data-testid='calendar-prev' and 'calendar-next' to month navigation chevrons in CalendarPage.jsx for spec compliance.
(OPTIONAL) Fix Recharts ResponsiveContainer width=-1 warnings by ensuring parent has min-width or using explicit aspect ratio.
(OPTIONAL) Backfill analytics_snapshots seed data for full 8 weeks so Analytics 'Weekly views' chart renders all bars.

RE-SEED needed: Tom's password is now 'newpass123' and Priya's is now 'newpass123' (forced-change flow was tested end-to-end). Marcus still has 'temp1234'. To reset: drop DB and restart backend (`mongosh $DB_NAME --eval 'db.dropDatabase()' && sudo supervisorctl restart backend`).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py insert_one() mutates input dicts with ObjectId; every POST that returns the inserted dict must exclude '_id' or use .copy() before insert. I fixed the two crashing endpoints (signup, team/invite) but reviewer should audit the rest (all other routes currently use find_one with {'_id':0} projection so they are safe).
server.py is 1042 lines — should be split into /routes subpackage (auth, team, analytics, tasks, etc.) for maintainability.
dashboard route manually checks permissions instead of using require_permission('dashboard_view') — inconsistent with rest of codebase (line 867-868). Should use dependency like others.
change-password endpoint: when must_change_password=True it silently skips current_password check — OK, but should also reject if new_password equals the temp password.
CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True — browsers will reject; should use explicit origin list in production.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (2 small fixes — exclude '_id' from signup & team/invite responses)

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all critical flows pass, 1 missing testid on calendar nav)

test_credentials: Jane=password123 (unchanged). Tom=newpass123 (CHANGED during test). Priya=newpass123 (CHANGED during test). Marcus=temp1234 (unchanged).
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Tests use TEST_ prefixed ephemeral records (signed up TEST_signup_* users, invited TEST_invite_* members — invited member is cleaned up; signup user is NOT auto-cleaned, will accumulate across runs).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite at /app/backend/tests/test_creatorhub_api.py is the regression baseline — run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_creatorhub_api.py -v`. If Tom/Priya passwords were reset, change USERS dict at top of file. The forced-password-change UI flow (Tom→Posts, Priya→Analytics) was verified end-to-end and is working. Permission enforcement (server-side 403s + sidebar filtering) is fully functional. Known non-bug: Analytics 'Weekly views' chart shows only last 2 weeks of bars because seed data is only 30 days.
